role_title: Alpha TIER Integration Services, Product Lead, Officer
secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with client implementation / solutions focus

level_signals: 5+ years of financial industry experience required
Officer-level title in State Street's job grade convention
Comp range $65K-$113,750 consistent with mid-senior individual contributor at State Street

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of financial industry experience across front office functionality along with middle (IBOR) and back office systems & services and Alpha data platform.
strong experience in project management, analysis, workflow design, data analysis, system flows and business architecture in order to create client solutions and execute implementation
candidate must possess skill in complex business data analysis with expertise in requirements gathering, business models and flows, documentation and data mapping
a proven ability to liaise between business/implementation and technology teams
Client engagement and expectation management across project life cycle
Responsible for designing solutions and onboarding new clients onto State Street's front-to-back platform

Drives the data conversion development lifecycle and methodology including requirements analysis, solution design, client acceptance, timeline creation, implementation, unit testing, deployment tools, and maintenance

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience integrating with other industry software providers to promote platform interoperability a plus (i.e. Bloomberg, CRD, BRS Aladdin, etc.)
Knowledge of SDLC & Agile Methodology and tools (i.e. JIRA) a plus

scope.surface: Alpha TIER Integration Services — State Street's front-to-back client servicing platform covering performance, risk, investment accounting, compliance, construction, trading, collateral, and data platform
scope.business_model: B2B institutional asset servicing and platform implementation
primary_metrics_inferred: Data conversion accuracy and timeline adherence
core_responsibilities: Designing solutions and onboarding new clients onto State Street's front-to-back platform
Leading workflow analysis, data analysis, requirements analysis for current and future state models
Creating and delivering internal and client-facing onboarding documents (proposals, playbooks, resource and governance models, cost and revenue models)
Managing complex client relationships and expectations with client stakeholders
Managing various stages of the onboarding lifecycle (solution design, integration and testing, implementation and BAU handover)
Becoming a product and platform SME on State Street's front-to-back platform
Supporting business development and sales through consultative client engagement and POCs
Driving the data conversion development lifecycle and methodology
Leading issue resolution and gap closure in data discrepancies

green_flags: Clear product surface and responsibilities described in detail
Officer-level title signals defined career ladder at State Street
Comprehensive benefits described including 401K match, medical, dental, vision
red_flags: Compensation range is wide ($65K-$113,750) and the lower bound seems low for a 'senior leader role' requiring 5+ years; may indicate wide band or location variance
JD labels role as 'senior leader' but Officer is a relatively junior title at State Street — potential title/seniority mismatch
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear for international applicants
Role appears onsite in Quincy, MA with no remote option mentioned — limits candidate pool

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'senior leader role' language, 5+ years requirement, Officer title, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and client description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false; no remote language in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (no mention in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The JD calls this a 'senior leader role' but the Officer title at State Street is typically a mid-level designation, not senior management — level interpretation is ambiguous.

The compensation range ($65K-$113,750) is unusually wide and the floor is low relative to 'senior leader' framing and 5+ years required experience; may reflect geographic pay bands or job family breadth.

It is unclear whether 'Client engagement and expectation management across project life cycle' under 'Education & Preferred Qualifications' is a hard requirement or preferred; treated as hard given it appears in a section that mixes both, and aligns with core responsibilities.
Work arrangement (onsite vs hybrid vs remote) is not explicitly stated in the JD body; location URL references Quincy, Massachusetts which implies onsite but hybrid is possible at State Street.
